description:
You are working as an IT-support technician who has been asked to give management a quick snapshot of how many ERROR events have occurred on a system today.  
A pre-gathered log file is already stored on the machine:

• Full path: /home/user/tickets/logs/system.log

Your job:

1. Read /home/user/tickets/logs/system.log.  
2. Count every line that contains the uppercase word “ERROR” (case-sensitive, whole word match).  
3. Write the total count to a brand-new file located at /home/user/tickets/analysis/error_summary.txt.

Required output format (exactly, including capital letters, the equal sign, and the trailing newline character):

ERROR_TOTAL=<number_of_errors>\n  

Examples of correct outputs:  
• If five errors are found → the file must contain exactly the single line  
  ERROR_TOTAL=5  
  followed by ONE Unix newline (line-feed, 0x0A).  
• No extra spaces, no additional lines, and no carriage-return characters.

You should accomplish this with a single terminal command (a one-liner).  
Do not delete or modify the original log file.  
When you are done, you can inspect /home/user/tickets/analysis/error_summary.txt on screen to confirm the correct value.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

Directory tree:
/home/user/tickets/
/home/user/tickets/logs/
/home/user/tickets/analysis/          (exists and is empty)

Pre-existing log file:
/home/user/tickets/logs/system.log
Contents (each line terminated with LF):
2023-04-18 10:15:23 INFO User login successful
2023-04-18 10:16:12 ERROR Database connection failed
2023-04-18 10:17:01 WARN Disk space low
2023-04-18 10:18:45 ERROR Timeout while fetching data
2023-04-18 10:20:10 INFO User logout
2023-04-18 10:22:05 ERROR Failed to send email

Ground-truth the judge will verify after task completion:

1. A new file /home/user/tickets/analysis/error_summary.txt must exist.
2. File permissions inherited from user umask (0644 is acceptable).
3. File contents must be exactly (including trailing newline):
ERROR_TOTAL=3\n
4. No other files under /home/user/tickets/analysis/ may be modified.
